articles.title: New Pathway Surfaced in Resin Bound Mulch in Norden, Rochdale
articles.excerpt: A new pathway surfaced in resin bound mulch has been completed in Norden, Rochdale, enhancing local outdoor spaces.
articles.content_html:
A new pathway surfaced in resin bound mulch has been completed in Norden, Rochdale. The project aims to improve accessibility and aesthetics in the area, providing a durable surface for pedestrians.

The resin bound mulch is designed to offer a smooth and safe walking experience, making it suitable for all users, including those with mobility challenges.

Local residents may benefit from the enhanced outdoor space, which could encourage more walking and outdoor activities in the community.

The completion of this pathway is part of ongoing efforts to upgrade public spaces in Rochdale, contributing to the overall improvement of the local environment.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Norden Village Summer Fete to feature duck race and family entertainment
articles.excerpt: Norden Village Summer Fete will take place on Sunday 19 July, featuring a duck race with cash prizes, family attractions, food, music and stalls.
articles.summary: The summer fete will begin at St Paul’s Parish Hall in Norden at 1pm, with the village duck race starting at 2pm.
articles.content_html:
Norden Village will host its Summer Fete on Sunday 19 July 2026, with an afternoon of family entertainment, food, music and the return of the village duck race.

The event will begin at 1pm at St Paul’s Parish Hall on Blackpits Road.

The duck race will start at 2pm, with cash prizes of £250 for first place, £100 for second place and £50 for third place.

Ducks cost £5 to enter and are available to purchase from participating businesses around Norden Village.

Visitors can also enjoy a bouncy castle, face painting, an ice cream van, a sweet stall, a bar, live music, food and other activities throughout the afternoon.

Event details

  • Date: Sunday 19 July 2026
  • Start time: 1pm
  • Duck race: 2pm
  • Location: St Paul’s Parish Hall, Blackpits Road, Norden
  • Duck entry: £5

Organisers have advised that there will be no parking available at the event and are asking local visitors to leave their cars at home where possible.

The fete is hosted by the Norden Environmental and Economic Village Association, known as NEEVA. Organisers have also stated that the duck race will not cause environmental harm.
